description_long: This object consists of a cluster of three stylized mushrooms, each with a thick, cylindrical stem and a broad, domed cap. The caps are a vibrant reddish-orange color, adorned with irregular white spots, reminiscent of the classic Amanita muscaria. The stems are a muted brown, with one mushroom displaying a lighter, beige ring near the base. The caps are slightly asymmetrical, with gentle curves and a matte finish, giving them a natural, organic appearance. The mushrooms are grouped closely together, with varying heights and cap sizes, suggesting a natural growth pattern. The underside of the caps is mostly hidden, but hints of a lighter, beige surface are visible on one mushroom.
description: A cluster of three stylized, red-orange mushrooms with white spots and thick brown stems, featuring natural curves and a matte finish.
description_view_0: Two large, red-orange mushroom caps with white spots dominate the view, with thick brown stems visible beneath.
description_view_1: The cluster reveals a smaller mushroom in the foreground, showing more of the beige stem and cap underside.
description_view_2: The largest mushroom cap is prominent, with a clear view of the beige underside and the thick brown stem.
description_view_3: The grouping of all three mushrooms is visible, with the caps overlapping and the stems partially obscured.
